method_atom: A large ensemble of mutually non-crossing one-dimensional stochastic trajectories whose collective 'reunion' (return-to-start) probability is expressed exactly as a matrix-integral partition function. As a single scale parameter is varied, the system undergoes a third-order phase transition in the large-N limit, and near the critical point the properly centered and scaled fluctuations follow a universal extreme-value distribution (with computable large-deviation tails); the same universal law also governs the maximum displacement of the ensemble.
object_atom: N non-intersecting Brownian motions on a finite line segment under periodic, absorbing, and reflecting boundary conditions — mapped onto two-dimensional continuum Yang-Mills gauge theory on a sphere with gauge groups U(N), Sp(2N), and SO(2N) — together with the maximal height of 'watermelon' Brownian excursions against a wall, where the extreme-value law realizes as the GUE/GOE Tracy-Widom distributions for the largest eigenvalue of a random matrix.
brief_reason: The non-crossing-paths-to-matrix-partition-function correspondence, the third-order transition, and the universal extreme-value (Tracy-Widom) fluctuations are the transferable machinery; the specific Brownian-walker boundary models, the sphere Yang-Mills gauge groups, and the GUE/GOE random-matrix identifications are the physics-specific objects.
